instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
How To Choose A Suitable ULIP Plan For Yourself
Overview of ULIP Plans
Unit linked insurance plans, better known as ULIPs, are market-linked financial instruments. They offer the investor the benefits of market-related wealth building as well as life insurance. ULIPs give you access to a number of investment funds, from which you can choose as per your risk appetite and financial goals. That’s not all; when you choose the ideal ULIP plan for investment, you have the option to switch between these funds multiple times.
This versatility may make ULIPs a preferred investment option for many. Let us delve a little deeper to learn about the various aspects of ULIPs and also find out how to choose ideal ULIP plan.
Types of ULIP Plans1: Typically, ULIPs can be classified into 2 categories. While both these types offer life insurance and market-linked returns, they have some distinctions too:
Higher of Sum Assured or Fund ValueIn case of the death of the insured, either the sum assured or the fund value is payable to the nominee, depending on what is higher. Let’s take an example: an insured has a ULIP with a sum assured of ₹40 lakhs. In the first year, the fund value is ₹10 lakhs. If he happens to die within the policy tenure, the nominee will receive a death benefit of ₹40 lakhs. In case the insured dies after 15 years, when the fund value is ₹80 lakhs, the nominee will receive ₹80 lakhs.
Sum Assured + Fund ValueIn Type 2 ULIP, the death benefit equals the sum assured and the fund value. Taking the example above, the payout in the first year will be ₹50 lakhs (₹40 lakhs + ₹10 lakhs). In case of the death of the insured in the 15th year, the payout will be ₹1.20 crores.
Steps to Invest in ULIP Plans in India:As there are a number of options when it comes to choosing the ideal ULIP plan for investment, it is essential that you choose your steps carefully.
Build an investment strategy for ULIPs:Any investment that you make has to suit your financial goals. With ULIPs, too, you need to see that you have the freedom to manage your financial portfolio. You need to decide your coverage amount, which investment to opt for, etc. and then choose the fund accordingly.Tip: For enhanced coverage, you can also opt for add-ons such as Accidental Death Benefit, Waiver of Premium Benefit, and Family Income Benefit, to name a few.
Look for a ULIP with multiple fund options¹⁰One of the many advantages of investing in a ULIP is that you get access to market-linked returns. Therefore, you may want to choose a policy that offers a wider range of fund options. Tip: In case you want or foresee a fluctuation in the market or for any other reason, you have the option to switch your funds and change your existing asset allocation. Hence opting for a ULIP with a wide variety of funds in different asset classes with a variety of investment objectives would provide you with higher flexibility.
Keep an eye on the charges¹⁰It is important to be completely aware of the cost structure and the charges when investing in a ULIP.Tip: Remember to go through the charges and the policy wordings at the time of buying a ULIP to make a well-informed decision.
ULIP Fund Classification11:
With ULIPs, you have the choice to pick funds that are based on your risk appetite as well as financial needs:
Equity FundsWith equity funds, you invest typically in company stocks and market-linked equities.
Liquid FundsIn these types of funds, your money will be directed for investing to bank and cash deposits, and other money market instruments that are low on risk.
Debt FundsAs the name suggests, here you choose to invest your money in debt market instruments such as corporate bonds, government securities and other fixed-income instruments. This fund has the possibility of providing fixed income to your portfolio in the future.
Balanced FundsTo create a balance with this fund choice, your money will be divided to be invested in market-linked equity assets, stocks, shares, and fixed-interest products.
Bajaj Allianz Life ULIP Plans & their Comparison:
Take a look at the table below to get a glimpse at some of the ideal ULIP plan for investment by Bajaj Allianz Life:
How to Choose the Ideal & Suitable ULIP Plan in India for Yourself?
ULIPs are typically a long-term investment. Spending some time and effort when choosing one can help you make a well-informed decision. Here are 6 handy tips on how to choose ideal ULIP plan.
Assess your goalsWondering how to select ideal ULIP plan? The first thing you need to ascertain is, “What is ideal for you?” An investment that ideally suits your financial goals is a suitable investment. You need to make sure that the policy that you choose is in line with your goals and needs. If you are planning to build your savings for your child’s higher education invest in a child ULIP. in case you are planning to save for your retirement you can invest in a pension ULIP.
Analyse your risk appetite Knowing your risk capacity will be very helpful in choosing the right kind of investment funds. Try understanding the different types of fund options available, and analyse what is in line with your risk appetite.
Choose the coverage carefullyThe main aim of investing in a ULIP is probably securing the future of your loved ones, even when you are not around. Therefore, it is crucial to choose the insurance coverage carefully.
Understand the type of ULIP you chooseMake sure you understand the two types of ULIPs that are available. The coverage offered by Type 2 may be higher than Type 1 ULIPs, but at the same time, the premium may be higher too.
Read the fine printThe policy document will give you an insight into the ULIP. Make sure to go through the document thoroughly and reach out to your insurer in case of any queries. You also need to be aware of the various fees and charges that may be levied on your policy, at the time of policy purchase and later on.
Choose the right policy tenureYou should choose a policy term that is in line with your financial goals. Partial withdrawals in ULIPs are an added advantage. A little advance planning can help you get money when you need it.
Make sure the premium fits your budgetWhen thinking about how to choose ideal ULIP plan, it is also important to choose a policy that suits your pocket. Ensuring that the premium is affordable will help in timely payments without any hassle.
How to choose ULIP funds on the basis of risk appetite?
Your risk appetite can greatly affect your choice of ULIP funds.
Debt Funds:As a low-risk investor, debt funds may be more suitable for you. However, keep in mind that low risk often means low returns.
Equity Funds:Equity funds are more well-suited for investors who wish to take high risks in order to receive higher returns.
Balanced Funds:As the name suggests, balanced funds are suitable for investing for those who wish to keep balance in their ULIP portfolio. A mixture of debt and equities can bring the needed stability for an investor.
Common ULIP Charges You Should Know About
ULIPs come with several fees and charges. It is important to understand them. Take a look at the table given below:
Things to keep in mind while getting a ULIP plan:
When making an investment in ULIPs, keep these things in mind:
ULIPS offer plenty of flexibility on your investments. Choose carefully
Make sure you understand the risks of investing your money. The market-linked returns are known to be volatile
ULIPs come with a 5-year lock-in period
A part of the premium that you pay will be directed towards life cover
Make sure that you are aware of the different fees and charges that may vary from policy to policy
Depending on your insurer, you may have a free-look period that allows you to return the policy. The period may be from 15 to 30 days.
ULIPs usually allow partial withdrawal once the lock-in is over
The premium that you pay towards your ULIP can get you tax benefits under Section 80C subject to conditions u/s 80C are satisfied under old tax regime.
Risk factors involved in investing in ULIP policy:
When you invest in ULIPs, a portion of your premium is directed towards investments. These investments are spread across the stock market, and you can invest in debt, equity or balanced funds. While this may help you gain higher returns, it also subjects the investments to market risks.
As a ULIP policyholder, you need to ensure that the investments are in line with your risk appetite. Accordingly, you can exercise control over the type of funds you want to invest in. With the flexibility of fund switching, you can switch between funds, depending on your changing needs or market conditions. Therefore, even for risk-averse investors, ULIPs can turn out to be a preferred option.
How to switch/ redirect to the ULIP plan?
A major advantage of ULIPs is that you can switch between your investment funds as per the changing dynamics of the market. Through fund switching, you can switch the investment from one fund to another, within the same policy. You can do it online through your insurance web portal, or offline by visiting a nearby branch. Redirection means the allocation of future premiums to a different fund. It generally takes place at the time of policy inception.4
Conclusion:
Until a few years ago, many financial planners bashed ULIPs mainly because of the product’s high-cost structure. However, since 2018, a lot of investors renewed their interest in unit-linked insurance plans, pertaining to the newly improved low-cost ULIPs. During the pandemic, the affinity of the investors reached an all-time high. As per a survey conducted, about 92% of the total participants reported an increased admiration for ULIPs post the pandemic’s first wave5.
Apart from wealth creation, ULIPs assure life coverage. This dual benefit attracts investors across customer profiles. Long-term returns, fund-switching flexibility, and systematic savings make ULIPs a preferred investment product. Knowing the nitty-gritty will help you understand how to choose ideal ULIP plan and achieve your financial goals.
FAQs
Is ULIP tax-free after 5 years?The premium that you pay towards the ULIP allows tax benefits up to INR 1.5 lakhs under Section 80C,subjet to conditions stated therein under old tax regime. Switching between the funds is tax-free, and so is the death benefit6.  Under section Section 10(10D), for policies purchased before 1 Feb 2021, the maturity benefit can be tax-free8 subject to conditions mentioned therein are satisfied. For ULIP policies issued after 1 Feb 2021, if aggregate annual premium is less than or equal to Rs. 2.50 lakhs, proceeds from policy can be tax-free if conditions mentioned under Section 10(10D) are satisfied and for policies where aggregate premium exceeds Rs. 2.50 lakhs, gain from such policies will be  taxable as Capital Gain in the hands of recipient.However, if you surrender your ULIP before the 5-year lock-in period, then deduction claimed under Section 80C  will be treated as your income and will be taxed as per your income slab9in the year in which such deduction was claimed.
What are the investment options available in the ULIP plans?The ULIP plans allow you varied investment options, such as:
Equity Funds
Balanced or Liquid Funds
Money Market Instruments
Income, fixed-income, and bond funds.
Do the ULIP plans offer a wide range of fund choices?With ULIPs, you get a range of funds to choose from. You have the option to spread your money across a variety of funds depending on your risk appetite. Through fund switching, you can balance your portfolio, as and when required.
